Summary: The Community 1 in IIT Jammu's Electrical Engineering Department is led by faculty member Gaurav Varshney. This group consists of four PhD students (Chandranshu Gupta, Jaswinder Kour, Mahendra Kumar Gurve, and Rina Mishra) who are supervised by Dr. Varshney. The community focuses on various research areas related to cybersecurity, including anti-phishing, internet security protocols, email and web security, vulnerability assessment, threat modeling, network security, and the application of machine learning in cyber security.

Summary: The Community 3 in IIT Jammu's Electrical Engineering Department is led by faculty members Manoj Singh Gaur and Shaifu Gupta. They oversee the research of PhD students Pragti Jamwal and Rishika Kohli, who are working on various aspects of cyber security, including networks on chip, malware analysis, and simulation of streaming protocols. The group's research focuses on areas such as computer networks, operating systems, VLSI design, cloud computing, machine learning, and federated learning, with a specific emphasis on security issues in these domains.

Summary: This community in IIT Jammu's Electrical Engineering Department is led by faculty members Mrinmoy Bhattacharjee, Sarada Prasad Gochhayat, Sayantan Mukherjee, and Sumit Kumar Pandey. The group focuses on research areas including audio and speech processing, machine/deep learning, natural language processing, and cryptography, with a specific emphasis on security and privacy in distributed networks and systems. One PhD student, Abhilash Kumar Das, is associated with this community under the supervision of one of these faculty members.

reports.text:
Community 7 in IIT Jammu EE Department:
  Faculty: Sidharth Maheshwari
  ResearchArea: Genome Analysis, Hardware-Software Co-Design, Battery Management Systems, Machine Learning (Tsetlin Machines), Genome Analysis - We are embarking on an innovative venture to develop energy-efficient genome analysis solutions through hardware/software co-design, specifically targeting low-power and affordable platforms. The focus is on genome assembly and downstream analysis using data generated from 2nd and 3rd generation sequencing technology. In additions, we intent to develop affordable hardware-based metagenomic and anti-microbial resistance monitoring pipelines, Battery Management System - Improving the battery management system (BMS) in electric vehicles (EVs) through the integration of machine learning (ML) via hardware/software co-design at the edge holds great potential for enhancing the overall performance of EVs. By leveraging ML in the BMS, several key areas can be targeted to achieve longer battery life, efficient charge capacity utilization
  Relationships: RESEARCHES_IN: 10

Summary: The Community 7 in IIT Jammu's Electrical Engineering Department is led by Faculty Member Sidharth Maheshwari. The group focuses on innovative research areas including Genome Analysis, Hardware-Software Co-Design, Battery Management Systems, and Machine Learning (specifically Tsetlin Machines). Their work involves developing energy-efficient genome analysis solutions and improving battery management systems in electric vehicles through the integration of machine learning at the edge.
reports.summary: The Community 7 in IIT Jammu's Electrical Engineering Department is led by Faculty Member Sidharth Maheshwari. The group focuses on innovative research areas including Genome Analysis, Hardware-Software Co-Design, Battery Management Systems, and Machine Learning (specifically Tsetlin Machines). Their work involves developing energy-efficient genome analysis solutions and improving battery management systems in electric vehicles through the integration of machine learning at the edge.

Summary: The Community 10 in IIT Jammu's Electrical Engineering Department is led by Faculty member Vinit Jakhetiya. The group consists of PhD students Ajeet Kumar Verma, Ambreen Bashir, Deebha Mumtaz, Rishabh Shukla, Sadbhawna, and Zeenat Fatima, who are supervised by Dr. Jakhetiya. This community focuses on research areas including Visual Quality Assessment, Perceptual Signal Modeling, Computer Vision, and Image Processing, among others, with applications in Video and Medical Processing, 3D Reconstruction, and the application of Machine/Deep Learning techniques.
